demographics.living_situation: lives at home with spouse; wheelchair-dependent and relies on partner for complex transfers and for bladder and bowel care

opening_complaint.verbatim_patient_text: My catheter hasn't drained all afternoon, my lower stomach is killing me, and I am freezing cold and shaking uncontrollably.
opening_complaint.stated_reason_for_presenting_today: The catheter bag has stayed empty for about six hours, urine is leaking around the tube, and over the same time I've spiked a fever with violent shaking and bad pain low down in my belly.

symptom_narrative.onset: about 6 hours ago the catheter stopped draining, and the fever and shaking came on over the same few hours
symptom_narrative.character: colicky, severe pain low in the belly with a full, bursting feeling; freezing cold with uncontrollable shaking and shivering
symptom_narrative.severity_patient_description: the pain is one of the worst I've had and the shaking won't stop
symptom_narrative.location_as_patient_describes: low in the middle of my belly, over my bladder
symptom_narrative.timing_pattern: constant and building since the bag stopped filling
symptom_narrative.what_makes_it_better: nothing so far; paracetamol a couple of hours ago barely touched the fever
symptom_narrative.what_makes_it_worse: it just keeps getting worse on its own
symptom_narrative.associated_symptoms_volunteered: urine is leaking around the tube instead of going into the bag and it smells really foul; I haven't been able to open my bowels for about four days
symptom_narrative.previous_episodes: I get urine infections fairly often because of the catheter, but I've never felt this sick with the shakes before
symptom_narrative.functional_impact: I can't get comfortable in the chair and my partner is having to do everything for me right now

past_medical_history.condition_as_patient_states: spina bifida from birth, with a repaired defect in my lower back and no movement or feeling in my legs
past_medical_history.how_managed: wheelchair user; specialist and community nursing follow-up

past_medical_history.condition_as_patient_states: bladder that doesn't work on its own, so I have a permanent catheter in all the time
past_medical_history.how_managed: long-term indwelling Foley catheter, changed by community nurses

past_medical_history.condition_as_patient_states: bowels that don't work properly and get badly constipated
past_medical_history.how_managed: partner assists with a bowel routine

past_medical_history.condition_as_patient_states: fluid on the brain as a baby, with a shunt tube in place

current_medications_as_reported.dose_as_patient_reports: took some about two hours ago for the fever

allergies_as_reported.reaction_described: I wear a medical-alert bracelet for it; my team always use latex-free gear on me

social_history_volunteered.relevant_life_context: lives with and is cared for by spouse; wheelchair-dependent; partner manages the catheter and bowel routine

objective_data_offered.value: about 39 degrees on our home thermometer just now
objective_data_offered.reliability_caveat: single reading on a home thermometer; taken by the patient's spouse

objective_data_offered.value: the leg bag has been empty all afternoon and urine is leaking out around the tube instead of draining; the leaking urine smells foul
he looks flushed and sweaty, is shivering and shaking, and is holding his lower belly and looks very distressed
his lower belly looks and feels swollen and full and he flinches when it is touched
objective_data_offered.reliability_caveat: carer impression, not a clinical examination
